metadata.summary: A shell AST library in Python

documentation:
# shasta

A python library with shell AST definitions for the [libdash](https://github.com/mgree/libdash) AST. It can be used to develop shell AST analyses and transfomations. All AST nodes support a `pretty()` method that extracts them as a shell script, and the library offers a [json_to_ast](./shasta/json_to_ast.py) module that creates an AST object from a JSON object.

It was originally part of [PaSh](https://github.com/binpash/pash) but was separated to allow others to build on it.

documentation: This version may give an unnecessarily long EOFFFF... (and therefore won't
match the OCaml output but it is still correct w.r.t. giving a fresh
marker, and uses less memory than fresh_marker above).

variables.name: shasta.print_lib.HEREDOC
documentation: like QUOTED, but _don't_ escape double quotes

classes.name: shasta.flags.OFlag
documentation: represents open flags present in the OpenFlag class
We don't need libbash as a dependency since we only use its classes
to hold already parsed data, but we still need its enums to interpret it.
Enums are IntEnums so they can be compared with libbash enums by their numeric value,

documentation: word is rhs of an assignment statement

documentation: don't perform tilde expansion on this word

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_NOASSNTILDE
documentation: don't do tilde expansion like an assignment statement

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_ASSNBLTIN
documentation: word is a builtin command that takes assignments

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_ASSIGNARG
documentation: word is assignment argument to command

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_HASQUOTEDNULL
documentation: word contains a quoted null character

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_DQUOTE
documentation: UNUSED - word should be treated as if double-quoted

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_SAWQUOTEDNULL
documentation: word contained a quoted null that was removed

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_ASSIGNASSOC
documentation: word looks like associative array assignment

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_ASSIGNARRAY
documentation: word looks like a compound indexed array assignment

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_ARRAYIND
documentation: word is an array index being expanded

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_COMPLETE
documentation: word is being expanded for completion

class_variables.name: shasta.flags.WordDescFlag.W_CHKLOCAL
documentation: check for local vars on assignment
represents word description flags present in the WordDesc class

a connection type enum - refer to execute_connection in execute_cmd.c

classes.name: shasta.flags.PatternFlag
documentation: a pattern flag enum, present in the CasePattern class

functions.name: shasta.ast_node.ast_node_to_untyped_deep
documentation: # This function takes an object that contains a mix of untyped and typed AstNodes (yuck)
# and turns it into untyped json-like object. It is required atm because the infrastructure that
# we have does not translate everything to its typed form at once before compiling, and therefore
# we end up with these abomination objects.
#    We need to fix this by properly defining types (based on `compiler/parser/ast_atd.atd`)
#    and creating a bidirectional transformation from these types to the untyped json object.
#    Then we can have all ast_to_ir infrastructure work on these objects, and only in the end
#    requiring to go to the untyped form to interface with printing and parsing
#    (which ATM does not interface with the typed form).

documentation: # This has to be here and not in print_lib to avoid circular dependencies
